top_signals.title: Iran warns ships it's 'unacceptable and dangerous' to transit the Strait of Hormuz without their approval
top_signals.summary: The stern warning underscores Tehran's resolve to retain control over the Strait of Hormuz and to resist transits that bypass its authorization.

top_signals.title: Africa’s Biggest IPOSparks Investor Frenzy in Dangote’s Nigeria
top_signals.summary: Africa’s richest person is sparking investor fever across Nigeria. Dangote's $40 billion IPO has people from all walks of life opening trading accounts out of FOMO, Ruth Olurounbi explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Despite Trump trade rhetoric, Asian investors keep betting on U.S.: Former House Majority Leader Eric Cantor
top_signals.summary: Cantor said that investment flows still have "so much" allocation to the U.S., because investors recognise that the country was still one with a "rule of law."

top_signals.title: Samsara tracking label targets $35B cargo theft problem
top_signals.summary:
Cargo theft costs U.S. businesses $35 billion annually. Samsara’s new tracking label delivers real-time freight visibility across any carrier with no system overhaul required.

top_signals.title: Trade Is Slowly Opening One of the World's Most Isolated Nations
top_signals.summary: Azerbaijan is working hard to coax Turkmenistan out of its shell and integrate Ashgabat into trans-Caspian trade routes. Azerbaijani leader Ilham Aliyev exchanged a variety of trade-related agreements with Turkmenistan’s titular head of state, Serdar Berdymukhamedov, during a June 22 ceremony in Baku. A joint statement issued after the ceremony highlighted an agreement to streamline “technical specifications” concerning the movement of goods and vehicles between the two countries, and another to harmonize “customs statistics…
